definitions.definition:
To locate
  1. To encounter or discover by accident; to happen upon.

parsedExamples.example: I found this shell on the beach.
She arrived home to find that the house had gone up in flames.
I found my car keys. They were under the couch.
I went looking for you in the bed we share, but tonight I found you not there.
I found you a new place to live

examples: I found this shell on the beach.
She arrived home to find that the house had gone up in flames.
I found my car keys. They were under the couch.
I went looking for you in the bed we share, but tonight I found you not there.
I found you a new place to live

definitions.definition: To encounter or discover by accident; to happen upon.

parsedExamples.example: I found this shell on the beach.
She arrived home to find that the house had gone up in flames.

examples: I found this shell on the beach.
She arrived home to find that the house had gone up in flames.

definitions.definition: To encounter or discover something being searched for; to locate.

parsedExamples.example: I found my car keys. They were under the couch.
I went looking for you in the bed we share, but tonight I found you not there.

examples: I found my car keys. They were under the couch.
I went looking for you in the bed we share, but tonight I found you not there.

definitions.definition: To locate on behalf of another.

parsedExamples.example: I found you a new place to live

examples: I found you a new place to live

definitions.definition: To discover by study or experiment directed to an object or end.

parsedExamples.example: Water is found to be a compound substance.
The researchers found the two variables to be strongly correlated.

examples: Water is found to be a compound substance.
The researchers found the two variables to be strongly correlated.

definitions.definition: To gain, as the object of desire or effort.

parsedExamples.example: to find leisure; to find means

examples: to find leisure; to find means

definitions.definition: To attain to; to arrive at; to acquire.

parsedExamples.example: Looks like he found a new vehicle for himself!

examples: Looks like he found a new vehicle for himself!

definitions.definition: To meet with; to receive.

parsedExamples.example: The proposal found little support within the government.

examples: The proposal found little support within the government.

definitions.definition: To point out.

parsedExamples.example: He kept finding faults with my work.

examples: He kept finding faults with my work.

definitions.definition: To decide that, to conclude that, to form the opinion that, to consider.

parsedExamples.example: I find your argument unsatisfactory.
I found it strange {sleeping - to sleep} in your house last night.

examples: I find your argument unsatisfactory.
I found it strange {sleeping - to sleep} in your house last night.

definitions.definition: To arrive at, as a conclusion; to determine as true; to establish.

parsedExamples.example: to find a verdict; to find a true bill (of indictment) against an accused person

examples: to find a verdict; to find a true bill (of indictment) against an accused person

definitions.definition: To supply; to furnish.

parsedExamples.example: to find food for workmen

examples: to find food for workmen

definitions.definition: To provide for

parsedExamples.example: He finds his nephew in money.

examples: He finds his nephew in money.

definitions.definition: To determine or judge.

parsedExamples.example: The jury finds for the defendant.

examples: The jury finds for the defendant.

definitions.definition: To successfully pass to or shoot the ball into.

parsedExamples.example: Peters finds Jinkins, who is running down the left wing.

examples: Peters finds Jinkins, who is running down the left wing.

definitions.definition: To discover game.
Anything that is found (usually valuable), as objects on an archeological site or a person with talent.
The act of finding.

parsedExamples.example: (Can we add an example for this sense?)

examples: (Can we add an example for this sense?)
